title: sideritis libanotica subsp. linearis
intro: Sideritis libanotica subsp. linearis, also known as white mullein or mountain tea, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and linear leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving respiratory health, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia and digestive issues. In herbal medicine, Sideritis libanotica subsp. linearis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of essential oils and ointments.
